original: Loitering in this barren section of corridor is a lone light gray-furred Jumper twiddling with an expensive-looking CODEX. One of her better hauls, no doubt. The lop-eared rabbit pays you no mind, focused to a fault on whatever it is she’s doing. Occasionally, the impressive bulge of a stiffening horse-cock throbs against her latex crotch-piece. An earphone cord wobbles between her screen and her eardrum, so whatever it is it’s probably an erotic adventure. Makes one wonder why she doesn’t just head into the lounge.\n\t\t\n\t\tSpeaking of, the sex-lounge can be found to the north, while the central loop beckons east and west.
context: <<POS:06630110>> textify(['\\n\\t\\tLoitering in this barren section of corridor'...])

original: A stack of heavy-duty cases weaves around the smooth corner of the central loop here, each one bearing the emblems of U.G.C Peacekeeper groups and PMCs like SagiTom. Mounds of them compete for space next to unsecured weapons, armors, helmets, and other gadgets. Either this booty is free for anyone who needs a hardware pick-me-up, or it’s all war materiel yet to be accounted for by whomever hauled it back. Upon a discriminating inspection, some of the laid-out weapons appear to be damaged, unloaded, or missing crucial components, and hardlight locks prevent the unauthorized from digging into the good stuff. Useless to someone like you, then.\n\t\t\n\t\tTreading west or south will ferry you further into the central loop.
context: <<POS:06631533>> textify(['\\n\\t\\tA stack of heavy-duty cases weaves around th'...])

original: Standing in the east section of the central loop, between the lounge and a room labeled ‘SLAVE QUARTERS’ by a fizzy lightscreen, it’s easy to feel a sense of unease. The sounds of heavy breathing fill your ears, as though someone were looming in your blind spot — nothing. Occasionally, collared pets will stride to and from the adjacent rooms, clean, or slathered. Yet when they pass, that sound is back. Every time you try to listen for it, find it, it stops... and when you gather your thoughts, it’s there to interrupt. No matter how hard you wince, squint, or shake your head, it lingers in the periphery.\n\t\t\n\t\tBefore you start hearing words and voices, you could head north or south up and down into the central loop, west to the lounge, or perhaps east into the slave’s quarters. The door does seem unlocked.
context: <<POS:06632520>> textify(['\\n\\t\\tStanding in the east section of the central '...])

original: Sizzling pink light-fuzz draws your attention to a large, airlock-style door. A neon-illuminated flag overhead bears the visage of a cobra with broad-white fangs dripping with what you presume to be poison. A gang hideout: observe the access panel, the nearness of cylirians, zel’rahn, and slyveren, each one dressed minimally to maximize their raw animal magnetism. Instead of kinetic firepower or energy-based weapons, they carry chem-grenades, hypoderm injectors, and some even have rows of stacked metal collars dangling from their belts like top-credit whores carrying dozens of unopened condoms in their garters.[var0| These are the Star Vipers, the most unpredictable and dangerous of the five gangs, responsible for the plurality of slaves brought into Zhèng Shi.]\n\t\t\n\t\tBe extremely careful here. West and North will carry you around the Ops Deck’s central loop.
context: <<POS:06633612>> textify(['\\n\\t\\tSizzling pink light-fuzz draws your attentio'...])

original:
Zhèng Shi has as many slaves as pirates and freebooters wandering around, and this room houses the most supremely sensual of the mentally broken, selected to cater for the five large crews running this base on their obedience, initiative, and genuine enthusiasm. You don’t have much time to think about the sticky, whirling design of this muggy room before a number of naked, cooing figures descend on you, assuming you to be their [pc.master] for the hour. Unbidden kisses pepper your [pc.arms]; fingers fall along your [pc.ass], [pc.thighs], and [pc.footOrFeet]; each one gently rocks you, desperate to guide you into mind-melting comfort and make the stresses of the greater universe disappear.

		They say there’s no rest for the wicked. ‘They’ haven’t been here yet.
		
		Before you get dragged into one of the many fluffy beds cloaked in sultry gloom, you could head back where you came from before things get too sloppy.
context: <<POS:06635215>> textify(['\\n\\t\\tZhèng Shi has as many slaves as pirates and '...])

original: You kind of have to bask in the unique comfort at the top of Zhèng Shi. It’s quiet; not too cold, not too warm, and well-lit. You feel like a cat under a bed surrounded by all these crates. When your out-of-place tranquility passes, you wonder if you have any business on the Operations Deck that would spare you an immediate return to the incessant clang of mining and construction or the hustle and glamor of an outlaw’s recreation.
context: <<POS:06636795>> textify(['\\n\\t\\tYou kind of have to bask in the unique comfo'...])

original: Standing here feels like being a fly on the wall of a top-of-the-line battleship. Pirates affiliated with Zhèng Shi’s ‘big five’ tread from the elevator to the pristine deck at large. Mouse-eared rogues veer towards the southern end of the hallway, avoiding any and all contact with their peers. None of them pay you any attention. There’s a lot of bellowing and laughter beyond the intersection north of you, and it contrasts disturbingly with the near-silent path to the south.
context: <<POS:06637542>> textify(['\\n\\t\\tStanding here feels like being a fly on the '...])

original: The central loop of the Operations Deck wraps around a wide communal area stuffed to capacity with pirates and slaves. Underfoot, a few liquor stains and thrown food trails into a roomy cafeteria adjoined by a lounge. Holo-screens projected between support columns proudly display the flashy emblems of all five gangs. Peeking into the clamor of the cafeteria shows you a number of laquines, thraggen, kaithrit, ausar, and other deplorables chowing down on stacked plates of food while naked pretty boys and apple-bottomed girls happily gorge themselves on whatever might be between their owners’ shuddering legs.\n\t\t\n\t\tTo the south is the cargo elevator that brought you here. North and east will take you further into the Ops Loop.
context: <<POS:06638296>> textify(['\\n\\t\\tThe central loop of the Operations Deck wrap'...])

original: A yellow-white galotian of immense size draws your attention in this corridor, her shifting goo sliding across the ceiling, the walls, and the floor, giggling as she slurps up the messes left by others. A fat, chrome collar closes around her neck and rests on her shoulders, as thick as her comically oversized bimbo lips. She pays you no mind, carrying out her endless task with a happy hum. What catches your eye are the objects floating within her bloated torso. You make out weapons, tools, and other mundane items twisting inside. Guess she’s also the mobile lost and found.\n\t\t\n\t\tHeading east will take you into the cafeteria room, while heading north or south will take you to the T-junctions and further into the secure areas.
context: <<POS:06639306>> textify(['\\n\\t\\tA yellow-white galotian of immense size draw'...])

original: There are more Jumpers sashaying to and from this neck of the deck than you’ve seen elsewhere. Most of the latex-wrapped pirates are hopping in tight formation. None of them cast so much as a mean stare in your direction, and, even more incredulously, they seem in control of their lusts. It should be obvious these laquines are Jumpers, but part of you is struggling to cope with it. They wear the uniform, the red lettering on their wide-flared thighs says it all... it’s difficult to believe these focused patrol-buns could be one and the same as the dopey, sweaty sluts you encountered in the mines.\n\t\t\n\t\tDown the west corridor is a yellow and blue-lit access passage through a security checkpoint of some alien schema, at the terminus of which is a large, high-security door. Just off to the side of that door is an entrance marked clearly with the Jumper’s emblems - a flag hangs overhead. To the east is the rest of the central loop. Up north is a lone door without any access panels.
context: <<POS:06640329>> textify(['\\n\\t\\tThere are more Jumpers sashaying to and from'...])

original: If it looks like a closet, it is. Unsurprising, but curiously spacious, decently quiet, and packed to the brim with expensive cleaning products. It seems that even pirates sometimes bother to keep their homes clean. Nothing in here is out of the ordinary: mundane chemicals, fancy brand-name varnishes, thick mops, rows and rows of air freshener and more cram the metal shelves.\n\t\t\n\t\tSouth will take you back to the central loop.
context: <<POS:06641603>> textify(['\\n\\t\\tIf it looks like a closet, it is. Unsurprisi'...])

original: Hair-raising silence envelops you heading south on this deck. Sure, it’s as clean as the rest of the place, and there’s the odd mop and bucket, a janitorial galotian, and a few things propped up against the wall... but there’s just nothing here. Well, except for a rather dim looking entrance at the far end. You can make out a large door and a bunch of software locks glimmering near it. You could head towards [flags.RATCOUNTERS |the rat thieves’ hideout |the ominous gate ] or turn back.
context: <<POS:06642504>> textify(['\\n\\t\\tHair-raising silence envelops you heading so'...])

original: You’re left squinting when you stare at what would be a simple airtight door completely painted over and scrabbled on with a bunch of, ahem, stylized rat drawings. There’s a blue-light panel by the entrance, its keys brightening at random. A tiny, swiveling camera stares imperiously from above, its dark eye watching your every movement.\n\t\t\n\t\tTo the east is a suspicious corner that seems connected to this place, warranting further investigation.
context: <<POS:06643289>> textify(['\\n\\t\\tYou’re left squinting when you stare at what'...])

original: The Sidewinder’s airlocks look much like its exterior: shiny, new, and engineered with a surprising degree of precision. Large square buttons operate the airlock’s settings, flanked by the sort of heavy duty, no-nonsense lever you’d expect to find on a military vessel. Automatic docking controls are positioned just below. The reverse wall houses manual linkages. Finally, a small panel on the outer door displays the airlock’s overall status - primarily that it is more or less wide-open, owing to compatible atmospherics on the outside. Handy.\n\nInside the inner door, to the east, lies an access corridor. You may exit the ship to the south.
context: <<POS:06645258>> textify(['The Sidewinder’s airlocks look much like its exter'...])

original: This north-south access corridor looks like it might run the whole length of the Sidewinder, and while it may be glitzy and brand-new, it’s also been decorated in typical garish, Jumper fashion. A promotion poster hangs from the east wall, framed in glowing neon. It depicts a buxom Jumper in a bright yellow jumpsuit and old-style bubble helmet, rocketing through an asteroid field with nothing but a hand blaster and the jets on her belt. Bold text at the bottom commands, “Jump into the fray... TODAY!”\n\nTo the north, the corridor widens into some kind of mess hall. It also stretches south, presumably toward the cockpit.
context: <<POS:06646876>> textify(['This north-south access corridor looks like it mig'...])

original:
The Sidewinder’s cockpit looks even flashier than the rest of the ship. Banks of programmable hardlight displays report on the conditions of dozens of subsystems. Black varmint-leather cushions lend the comfy bucket seats an air of luxury, ensuring that this magnificent beast’s crew can truly enjoy themselves while plundering the galaxy’s riches. The captain’s chair is the largest by far, sat behind an array of triple holodisplays, twin flightsticks, and two full keyboards. Judging by the setup, this vessel can be operated by a single pilot if need be, though a good crew would undoubtedly make it more effective.

As you’re looking it over, a twelve-inch hardlight horse-cock springs out of the chair, wobbling with simulated weight. Of course the Jumpers designed a ship with a dildo in the chair! How else would they take it for pervy joyrides? At least there appears to be a button to turn it off. You don’t, of course. The idea of a bunny squirming on that thing while she tries to navigate an asteroid field is simply too amusing.

The cockpit’s access hatch leads north.
context: <<POS:06648548>> textify(['The Sidewinder’s cockpit looks even flashier than '...])

original: The ship’s mess hall is a combination kitchen and dining room, complete with tables capable of seating up to twenty. The kitchen is everything you’d expect from hedonistic pirates. Gold trimmed protein synthesizers are neatly packed next to an ornate las-oven and a half-dozen other esoteric food preparation devices. A simple robotic servant trundles around with a touchscreen on its blank face listing suggested foods. At some point, its narrow, servile frame was modified to carry around a set of heavy sexbot knockers that jiggle dangerously with its every step. It appears oblivious to the fact that someone scrawled “Fill these puppies with whiskey!” across them.\n\nA wide access corridor intersects with the mess, stretching north toward the cargohold and engines and south up to the cockpit. A heavy doorway to the west gives the captain convenient access to food from his or her quarters.
context: <<POS:06651019>> textify(['The ship’s mess hall is a combination kitchen and '...])

original: Furnished with a heart-shaped bed and more throw pillows than any one planet should be allowed to contain, the captain’s quarters look more like a harem chamber than a place of rest. Discarded bottles of lube lie everywhere. The trashbin looks halfway overflowed with condoms. A pair of frilly panties hangs framed on the wall along with a signed picture of Nivas Oxonef, indicating the ultrapornstar herself once wore them. The nightstand is covered with used TamaniCorp gift cards, already scratched off to get at their valuable codes.
context: <<POS:06653009>> textify(['Furnished with a heart-shaped bed and more throw p'...])

original: The corridor stretches north and south past quite a few rooms for crew members, every single one of them locked down for the time being. You suppose that once a proper crew manifest is loaded into the console, you’d be able to get inside, but right now, you have bigger fish to fry. The used condom on the floor gives one suggestion as to why these chambers were sealed off while the ship is in drydock, not that the pirates on this station seem to give one whit about finding somewhere private to bang.
context: <<POS:06653911>> textify(['The corridor stretches north and south past quite '...])

original: A lone chair sits against this passage’s northern terminus, next to a stack of magazines. Doors flank it to either side, offering access to “Engine Room A” and “Engine Room B.” The latter is presently sealed for testing, according to the message on its terminal. The former is closed but unguarded. This single seat must be where the head mechanic comes to take a break from his job, judging by the hot-rodded spaceships and burly beefcakes his (or her?) chosen literature displays. You spot the following headlines at a glance: Ion Engine Tuning Made Easy! Cum Like Tank Kannon With This One Simple Trick! Pecs: The Milky Manjugs of the Future! Anal: How Big Is Too Big? Light Drive Showdown: Which Model Covers the Most Parsecs in a Sec?
context: <<POS:06654953>> textify(['A lone chair sits against this passage’s northern '...])
